All right everyone, welcome back. I hope you had a good time during that session and that you guys got a lot of value from it. So what I wanna kinda transition to now is, I wanna start transitioning from, we talked about earlier where we talked about who's your dream client, where are they at, what's the bait you're gonna get with them and then where are you gonna send them. So now we're gonna be talking about kind of the bait that we're gonna use to grab them and then how we're gonna send them up to our highest level offerings, okay? And so for those of you guys who've gone through the book or the training you know, I've talked a lot about value ladders, okay? And so a value ladder is basically somebody comes into your universe and you gotta provide value to them. If they receive value, they're naturally gonna want more, they're gonna wanna ascend up and get more stuff, okay? And that's just kinda how humans work, right? Like for example, we got to eat, let's say we go to this great sushi place and we eat it and it's just amazing, what do we want? We want more of that, we wanna come back, we wanna take sushi classes from these guys. We wanna like, we naturally wanna ascend up and get more and more. And so when we're structuring our businesses, that's the goal is to like at every level, every interaction somebody has with us, we wanna give them so much value that like, wow, I need more of this, we wanna cause addictions in our customers, okay? I always think about that, it sounds kinda goofy and kinda weird, but if you can cause an addiction with your customers, they keep wanting to come back over and over and over again, that's like the best thing in the world, okay? And so we do that through funnels. Now inside the dot com secrets book, I share a lot of different types of sales funnels. And I don't wanna talk about all of them today because some of them are more advanced funnels, some of them they're using different, like more unique situations, but there's three core funnels that we use typically in almost all of our businesses. And for you guys, you're probably at some point or another gonna use all three of them, but to begin with, I want you guys to pick the one that's gonna make the most sense for you to start with, okay? Sometimes some businesses, and I'm gonna walk you through the three kinds, the first type of funnel is typically kind of on the bottom of the value ladder and it's called a trip wire funnel. And this is where you're selling low ticket things to, again, provide value and make somebody want to ascend up, okay, in the middle of the value ladder, we typically sell through something we call the perfect webinar. And the perfect webinar is usually you're selling products more expensive, somewhere from $300 up to $2,000 or $3,000. And in the back, we have a high ticket funnel where we're selling super expensive anywhere from $5,000 to $100,000, a million dollars or more, okay? And so that's kind of the process. Now, I used to teach people, I used to believe that you had to bring someone into the value ladder at the bottom and then ascend them up. But the reality that I've seen, especially recently over the last year and a half or two years, is that each of these types of funnels kind of is a value ladder in and of itself, if that makes sense, okay? For example, like a trip wire funnel, and I'll show you as a bunch of examples of this, typically a trip wire is like a really low ticket thing. It might be free plus shipping or it might be $7, something really, really low. And so somebody buys that and it's an easy transaction for them. They buy it and also they get this thing, they get some value and then they're gonna naturally want to send up, okay? So sometimes we'll drive traffic to a trip wire. But sometimes, like for example Liz, when she came into our coaching program, she didn't have anything to do these things. And for her, it made the most sense to start with the webinar. And so she did a webinar. Now what's interesting is her webinar is selling a $1,000 product. You might think, who's gonna come and come to Liz and they never met her before and also give her $1,000? Well, typically you won't because, because again, she doesn't provide a value. So the sales process changes a little bit, okay? So for here with the perfect webinar, somebody comes, they register for webinar for free, they sit on the hear her talk for 60, 90 minutes where she sells her product in that process. She's providing tons of value and so because of that, because of the sales process, she's able to sell a $1,000 thing because the webinar, that process provides the value. So you can drive traffic directly here. Same thing with the high ticket funnel. We drive people all day long from cold traffic to a high ticket funnel and it works because the way the sales process works where it's warming someone up and they're getting value during the sales funnel. And when they do that, they're more, they're able and they're more likely to spend really, really expensive things, okay? And so it's important to understand that, that you don't have to start with this. You don't have to start with the, you pick the one that makes the most sense for your type of business and then you kind of craft that one out first. And so we're gonna, and one of my goals for this for you guys is to figure out which one of these I might create first and then we're gonna laser focus in on that, okay? So again, tripwires are typically selling lower ticket things, anywhere from, excuse me, from $0 free plus shipping type things up to, you know, $10, $20, something like that. Perfect webinar, you're selling anywhere from 300 bucks to two or 3000 and high tickets usually, two or 3000 up to as big as you can, as big as your imagination will allow you to dream. And so that's kind of the game plan. So those are the three different funnels. So I'm gonna kind of walk you guys through them all really quick. So the first is called a tripwire funnel. And in the book, I never called it a tripwire, but recently it's been kind of nicknamed this, a guy named Perry Belcher kind of came up with that term tripwire and the industry's been calling it out. So we're gonna call it that for this workshop just so you guys know kind of, as you learn more online, people reference it and call it that. So we're gonna call it that for this training. So tripwire typically is, the goal of a tripwire is a product that you're trying to get somebody to come in, okay, at a low barrier. For example, I told you guys earlier today about the dotcom secrets labs book when we created that. Initially my goal was to sell it for two or 300 bucks. I was gonna do a webinar and try to sell it. But I thought, you know what, like, if I just gave this away for free, like everyone would get it. And if they get it and they get value from it, they're gonna love it. Okay, remember, because when we first got it, I gave it out to a couple of people and people went crazy for it. And they told me, it's on my desk. I had tons of people say, like, I got the book and it's in my bathroom and I read it every time I go to the bathroom, which is totally creepy. But that's the good thing, right? I want every time someone's going to the bathroom to think about Russell Brunson. Every time they're sitting there they're thinking about Russell Brunson, right? And so my thought was like, I want my best thing in the hands of as many people as possible. And so against all logic, I said, let's just give it away for free. And it was free plus shipping. So they basically came in, they put a credit card in, they pay, I can't remember, eight or nine bucks for shipping and handling. We mail it out to them and boom, they get my best product in their hands. Okay, that's the first thing. Now, in the next presentation I'm gonna walk through what happens after that, because this is where the magic happens, because you can't sell things for zero dollars and keep staying in business that long. So there's a bunch of magic that happens behind the funnel we'll cover in the next session. That's kind of what a tripwire is. It's a low ticket thing to get somebody and to bring him into your world really quickly. So here's a couple examples. This is the Dotcom Secrets Labs one. Again, this little tripwire funnel, I can't even tell you, is brought in over 10,000 new of my dream clients into our business. On the back of this we sold over, I say over 150 people, we sold our 10,000 hour coaching program and 36 people bought my 25,000 hour program from this. People are like, Russ, why did you get this book away for free? I'm like, because based on my numbers, I don't know what it is, it's the top of my head, $2.3 million we made from people who got this, got a ton of value and then wanted more. So that's the Dotcom Secrets Labs, one of our tripwire funnels. This is one of my friends' companies called Survival Life and they went to China and found these little credit card knives for like 30 cents in China. He bought a whole bunch of them and made them free plus shipping. I think you pay 295 shipping and handling. He put this out there, first month, excuse me, first month on Facebook, they sold 130,000 of them. She's got 130,000 buyers onto their email list and then Facebook kicked them off because they didn't want to sell knives. They took it out, they've been driving traffic 10 other places. But I think last time I heard they had sold free plus shipping in these knives, like 400,000 or 500,000 of these knives. So 400,000 or 500,000 buyers have come into their world. I was talking to Mike yesterday, Mike, one of his companies buys traffic from these guys and just for you to buy an email to their customer list is 22,000 bucks. And they sell an ad every single day. And so they're getting so many customers coming in because of this really unique thing. So I don't want you to think like, in your business, what do you have that could bring someone in like that? Do you have a book? Do you have a CD? Do you have a knife that's gonna bring your dream client in? Okay, for these guys, they're in the survival market. Their dream client would love a credit card knife that's gonna get through the TSA. In fact, their ads are so funny. You're like, the TSA won't even catch your, you won't even know you're packing your knives on the plane. Like all these weird things, but their dream customer, like that's what they want to be able to like have a knife with them on the airplane in case terrorist attack, right? And so it brings their dream client in, okay? This is another one that I thought was really unique. This is also the same guys that own survival life on this one. They started a company where they do suits, custom fit suits. And so they're trying to think like, how do we sell like super expensive custom suits online? Okay, it's not that easy, right? Like how would you, like, it's not that sexy. I think typically if you want to buy a suit, where are you gonna go? You're gonna go down to the tailor and get fitted and all that kind of stuff. And so they're like, well, how do we do this? And I said, well, we know one thing that not everybody who wears a suit has cuff links, but everybody who buys cuff links wears suits. So they found these really cool cuff links, got them sourced in China, give them away for free plus shipping, and they got hundreds of thousands of people who now get the free things on Facebook. Boom, now they can start selling them their suits, shirts, tie, all this other stuff, and they built a huge company based on that. Is that cool? This is one of my buddies, this is his supplement company. And he doesn't even charge shipping, he just, again, wanted to give value. So he's got this page that just says, hey, if you want a free sample pack, let us know where to ship it, no credit card required, just give me your address, we'll ship it out to you. And so you fill that out, you put it in, and he ships you this little thing in the mail. And it's really interesting, I, every time I call him, I said, why don't you try to sell me anything? He doesn't, afterwards, on the next page, he doesn't try to sell me anything, he doesn't email, he just gives away free stuff and then hopes you come back. And his company has grown 20% every single month since he's launched this thing. They're making insane amounts of money just by giving away free stuff. You get a sample and you get addicted and they keep coming back. We call it the drug dealer clothes, someone said that earlier. So that's kind of, that's like a cool way with the supplement. So you guys have a supplement you can use that's gonna attract your dream customer, your dream client in. This is one of my friends, because the examples I've seen you so far have all been free plus shipping type products, but they don't have to be free plus shipping. This is one of my friends, he's in the real estate market and he's teaching people how to flip real estate. So he wrote this little report called how to flip houses from your cell phone and he charges $7 for this report, it's like an e-book, and he makes insane amounts of money from people who wanna learn how to flip real estate buying this little report. So that's kind of what a tripwire is. It's a low ticket thing that is designed and crafted to attract your dream client. If you look at this book you guys, if you watch the marketing we've done behind this, everything about this was designed to get my dream client. I could have put something like, how to make a million dollars on the internet? Who's that gonna attract? The dude that I don't wanna be working with. We made this, the underground playboy for growing your company online. I got Tony Robbins in her testimony, Dan Kennedy, like this is bait that was created to attract my dream client. When we launched this book, we're not charging for it. It's free plus shipping, because I wanna get as many of these people in my world as possible, and then from there I can do all the rest of stuff, send them up, sell them other products and other services. So if you guys, I want you to think in your company, and again everyone's gonna be different, some of you guys, the tripwire style final's gonna be the one you're gonna focus on first, some of you guys might not be, but some of you guys that's gonna be the one you wanna do first. So start thinking like what do you have, what could you create that's gonna bring your dream person in, okay? And it can be products you get from China, it can be information you create, it can be DVDs, it can be a report, right? There's a million different ways to do it. It's just what can you craft, what can you create? What's the bait? We showed you guys earlier, it's the bait. What's the bait that's gonna get your dream client to bite? If you can create that, boom, you've got him. So that's what tripwire funnels are. The second type of funnel, which is the webinar funnel. Now a webinar funnel is typically, I'm selling a more expensive product. Like I said, somewhere from $300 up to a couple thousand and the way that a webinar funnel works is somebody comes and it's more like a live event, okay? When I got started in this business 12 months ago, 12 years ago, it was funny, like people were doing a lot of live events. This was how everything worked. So you pay a couple hundred bucks, you fly out to San Diego, you go to an event, they have a whole room of people there and they get someone come up on stage, they would speak and teach about something and then they'd sell something and everyone would run to the back of the room and give them money and that was kind of the model. And I remember the first event I ever went to, I remember seeing this and the first speaker got up on stage and he sold and everyone's running to the back of the room and I remember like doing the math in my head, the guy was selling a $2,000 package and I'm watching people in the back and I'm two, four, six, eight, 10, 12, I'm like, that guy just made $65,000 in an hour and the next speaker gets up, he does a little pitch and then he says, closes and everyone starts running back and he was selling like a four, $5,000 package and I'm like, five, 10, 15, 20, I made 80 grand in an hour. I remember looking at it, I was like, I gotta learn how to do what those guys just did. Like that was the most, these guys are making more in an hour than my parents would make an entire year. I remember being so blown away and so for me, I started learning how to sell things from stage and things like that and over the last 10 years, that's morphed where less people are doing live events because it's so expensive to get people to show up but now they're doing virtual events and the process that we're doing is, and some of you guys, hopefully if you went through the book or the training, I showed you guys my perfect webinar script which is a script we built to actually how to do the presentation, but the perfect webinar funnel is crafted and created to sell more expensive things. Because again, someone's coming in, they're hearing you speak for 60 to 90 minutes, that's when they get tons of value and then that's why it's okay and you're able to sell them at the end of it. And so for some of you guys, this is gonna be the funnel that makes the most sense for you if you have more of a high-ticket, more expensive type product. This is an example of one of my webinars. It's called Funnel Hacks. We launched this thing, I think four months ago. During the presentation, we sell a $1,000 product. In the first three weeks, we did a million dollars in sales. We're over two million right now and it just makes a ton of money. And it's a really cool process. So what happens is we drive people this page, they click on the button, they register for webinar, and then typically what we'll do is we'll do these webinars on a Thursday. So Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, we're driving traffic this webinar, they register, then Thursday, they show up to the webinar, we get on there, I do my little presentation, I teach a Friday bunch of value at the end to make a special offer and a percentage of people will always buy at the end. And it's just amazing how much money you can make with these. So this is one of our webinar funnels, it's called Funnel Hacks. This is Liz Benny's, so I showed you guys the video from Liz earlier. So this is the funnel we decided to start with with her. I remember she was like, first, should I make a free plus shipping thing? Should I, what should I do? And I'm like, no, with you, you want to sell about a thousand dollar product for your business, for what you're trying to create, the webinar is the right type of funnel. So she built her webinar funnel out, she created her webinar, she followed the perfect webinar script that's in the book to a T. And it's kind of a funny process. She started driving traffic to it, at first it didn't work and she had to tweet a couple things. And now, every time she does the webinar, every Thursday she does the webinar and she crushes it and then she'll vox me afterwards and she'll send me the numbers just to kind of, because for me I love like, nothing gets me more excited than seeing people's success. And so she'll vox me like, hey, there's something to sell as we got. And it's crazy, cause every single week, she'll get anywhere from 800 to 1500 people to register for this webinar. By buying ads, she's spending between $1 and $3 to get someone to register for the webinar. They say she gets 1500 people, about a third of those people show up live, she does the webinar live, and when she's on there, she'll get anywhere from 10 to 15% of people to buy. And so when she's on the live webinar, it's so funny because she'll do the webinar and then she'll text me afterwards, hey, made 23,000 bucks and boom, I'll see that text come in and then she has a follow-up sequence that she'll take people through and the next day she's like, boom, up to 33,000. And the next day I'm up to 55,000 and then whatever ends up being at the end of the week. And it's crazy, every single week, by doing this little webinar process, we're literally, she just, I mean, she's a hard worker, but she doesn't have to do much all week long. People are driving traffic for her, she gets people in, webinar happens on Thursday, she shows up, does her show, our presentation and she's done and she's making $40, $50, $60,000 a week, week in and week out with this webinar funnel. Okay, so if you guys are selling a product that kind of fits in that price range, this webinar funnel is just amazing. It provides tons of value, it builds your list really, really quick and it's an easy and amazing way to sell things like that. One of the cool things, and as Chad is someone here, Chad's a chiropractor, you guys know, and he's been doing his thing locally and one of the cool things about watching him with his company is he's been taking that from this local thing, like how do I serve people when I'm not just bound by my geographic location? Like how do I break out of that? So he's taking his knowledge and his skills now and turning it into a perfect webinar where now he can sell $300,000, $500,000 packages to people all around the world and it's just cool to see how you can take your expertise. Because so many of us, we put these limits on ourselves, right? Especially the local business owners here. Like here's my geography, I can attract people around here, but you've got skills that you can serve people from everywhere. So how do you change this and tweak that? And the webinar is a way that you can really take your message out to the masses. So that's the webinar funnel. And the last is the high ticket funnel. And this is where you're selling really, really expensive stuff. Anywhere from a couple thousand bucks up to, again, as big as your mind can dream. And so that's where the high ticket funnel is. And I'll show you kind of our high ticket funnel. This is what it looks like for us. And I've got a bunch of different variations of this. This is one, there's a video Tony Robbins talking about me. I've got one with Liz, I've got one with a whole bunch of other people talking about their experience with me, the result they had. And afterwards there's a button that says, if you want to learn more, you got to apply. They click on application button, they apply. And then from there, there's a whole process behind the scenes where they get a phone call and they get sold into our hiring coaching programs. And so for you guys, if you're selling coaching or consulting or high ticket things, things are expensive that are hard to sell just on a webinar and in a group selling situation, you got to sell it more one on one. So I found when you go over about two to $3,000, most people to be willing to pull a credit card out and call their bank and increase their limits, they can spend more than two or $3,000. It typically doesn't happen in a group setting where I'm just pitching it and everyone signs up. It typically takes more touches to get someone to do that. So we do that through a high ticket funnel where there's a phone call afterwards, someone's actually talking to them on the phone and selling the more expensive thing. Okay, so that's kind of what the high ticket one looks like. This is another guy. He's inside of ClickFunnels. I just met him, got to know him in the last couple of weeks. This is his high ticket funnel. Right now they're doing about $300,000 a month on it and it's very similar to the way that we structure our high ticket funnel as well. He's selling, he does a, every month, he does a four day. He calls it his warrior week and he gets people to pay $10,000 to come to his warrior week and he beats the crap out of them for four days. And then at the event he sells them a $50,000, like life or a year long version of him beating the crap out of them. It's pretty cool and he's crushing it. But it's again a high ticket thing. So if you've got something where you're thinking like high ticket expensive, things like that, then the high ticket funnel is gonna be the one for you. So the first thing I want you guys to kind of think about right now is for your business right now, the one we're gonna be focusing on kind of over this process, which will make the most sense for you. Are you gonna be starting with some kind of like trip wire funnel? Does that make the most sense for your business? Or do you need to focus more on like a perfect webinar type funnel? Or do you wanna work more on like a high ticket type funnel? Which one makes the most sense for your business? I want you to take a second, kind of mark down which one makes the most sense for you. And then we're gonna go around the room and kind of talk about which one you chose and why you think it's gonna be a good fit for your company.
